front.title: A YANG Data Model for Optical Impairment-aware Topology

abstract.t:
Provisioning an optical connection requires that path continuity, resource availability, and impairment constraints be satisfied in order to determine viable paths through the network. This process is known as Impairment-Aware Routing and Wavelength Assignment (IA-RWA) in Wavelength Switched Optical Networks (WSONs) and as Impairment- Aware Routing and Spectrum Assignment (IA-RSA) in Spectrum Switched Optical Networks (SSONs).
This document defines a YANG data model for impairment-aware Traffic Engineering (TE) topologies in optical networks. The model augments the technology-agnostic YANG data model for TE topologies and provides read-only topology information, including optical impairments. Such information can be used, for example, by a Path Computation Engine (PCE) to compute optically feasible paths prior to connection establishment.
